魏.origin: 魏 has ghost 鬼 on the side; it is the name of the Wei kingdom and a family name.

sentence.en: The state of Wei was in the north.

魔.origin: 魔 puts hemp 麻 over ghost 鬼; it means a demon or magic.

sentence.en: He can do magic.

鲁.origin: 鲁 joins fish 鱼 and a mouth's words; it means dull or rash, and is the old name for Shandong.

sentence.en: Do not be rash when doing things.

鲍.origin: 鲍 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means abalone, and is also a family name.

sentence.en: Abalone is very expensive.

鲜.origin: 鲜 joins fish 鱼 and sheep 羊; it means fresh and tasty.

sentence.en: These fruits are very fresh.

鳃.origin: 鳃 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means the gills a fish breathes with.

sentence.en: Fish breathe with their gills.

鳄.origin: 鳄 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means a crocodile or alligator.

sentence.en: Crocodiles live in the river.

鳍.origin: 鳍 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means a fish's fin.

sentence.en: Fins help the fish swim.

鳔.origin: 鳔 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means the swim bladder that helps a fish float.

sentence.en: The swim bladder helps the fish float.

鳖.origin: 鳖 has fish 鱼 at the bottom; it means a soft-shelled turtle.

sentence.en: There is a soft-shelled turtle in the pond.

鳞.origin: 鳞 has fish 鱼 on the side; it means the scales on a fish.

sentence.en: There are scales on the fish's body.

鸠.origin: 鸠 has bird 鸟 on the side; it means a turtledove or pigeon.

sentence.en: A turtledove perches in the tree.

鸢.origin: 鸢 has bird 鸟 at the bottom; it means a kite, a bird of prey.

sentence.en: Irises are blooming in the garden.

鸣.origin: 鸣 joins mouth 口 and bird 鸟; it means the cry a bird makes.

sentence.en: The little bird is chirping.

鸽.origin: 鸽 has bird 鸟 on the side; it means a pigeon or dove.

sentence.en: There are many pigeons in the square.

鹅.origin: 鹅 has bird 鸟 on the side; it means a goose.

sentence.en: There is a white goose by the river.

鹤.origin: 鹤 has bird 鸟 on the side; it means a crane, a tall elegant bird.

sentence.en: The white crane stands in the water.

鹫.origin: 鹫 has bird 鸟 at the bottom; it means a vulture or condor.

sentence.en: The vulture flies in the sky.

鹰.origin: 鹰 has bird 鸟 at the bottom; it means an eagle or hawk.

sentence.en: The eagle flies very high.

鹳.origin: 鹳 has bird 鸟 on the side; it means a stork, a tall wading bird.

sentence.en: The stork looks for fish by the water.

鹿.origin: 鹿 draws a deer with antlers, legs, and a tail; it means deer.

sentence.en: There is a little deer in the forest.

麝.origin: 麝 has deer 鹿 on top; it means the musk deer.

sentence.en: Musk has a very strong scent.

麦.origin: 麦 pictures grain ready to be harvested; it means wheat.

sentence.en: The farmers grow wheat.

麻.origin: 麻 shows hemp plants 林 under a shed 广; it means hemp, and also numb.

sentence.en: This matter is very troublesome.

黎.origin: 黎 means many or numerous; it is also used for dawn, as in 黎明.

sentence.en: At dawn the sky brightens.

黏.origin: 黏 has millet 黍 on the side; it means sticky.

sentence.en: The snail leaves behind sticky slime.

黔.origin: 黔 has black 黑 on the side; it means black, and is the short name for Guizhou.

sentence.en: He lives in southern Guizhou.

默.origin: 默 shows a dog 犬 in the dark 黑; it means silent and quiet.

sentence.en: She reads quietly.

鼎.origin: 鼎 pictures a three-legged bronze cauldron used in ancient times.

sentence.en: Thank you for your great help.

鼻.origin: 鼻 has self 自 on top, which once drew a nose; it means nose.

sentence.en: The elephant's nose is very long.

鼾.origin: 鼾 has nose 鼻 on the side; it means to snore loudly.

sentence.en: Grandpa's snoring is very loud.

齐.origin: 齐 shows things grown to the same height; it means even, neat, and together.

sentence.en: The bookshelf is very tidy.

齿.origin: 齿 draws teeth inside the mouth; it means teeth.

sentence.en: I brush my teeth every day.

龚.origin: 龚 has dragon 龙 on top; it once meant to give, and today is mostly a family name.

sentence.en: Her surname is Gong.

龟.origin: 龟 draws a turtle with its shell and tail; it means turtle or tortoise.

sentence.en: The turtle walks very slowly.
